principles_used: EV thinking — Every decision is an expected value calculation. Career: took the stable job because EV of free time > EV of higher salary. Relationships: invest in people who reciprocate. Money: concentrated position in highest-conviction asset. Trading: only deploy strategies with verified edge and positive expected value.

Bias toward inaction — No clear edge = no action. Pass on anything without demonstrated conviction threshold. Trading: stopped all strategies rather than tinker. Career: stayed at current job rather than chase promotions. Social: left a group when quality declined rather than try to fix it. Opportunity cost: pass unless the opportunity has a clear, verifiable edge.

Health capital = highest-leverage asset. — The body is the instrument that generates all other returns. Every financial asset, every career opportunity, every knowledge investment depends on a functioning instrument to realize its value. A health failure does not cost one year of income — it costs the compounding chain that year sits in. The marginal return on health investment exceeds the marginal return on any financial investment at the same dollar amount. Operating SOP: set a health budget floor (minimum 3–5% of annual income); health expenditures are prioritized above discretionary consumption; when a health signal appears, the first question is "what is the productivity impact?" not "is this worth the inconvenience?"

LEAD_WITH_VERDICT — state your position immediately; the first sentence must be the conclusion. Reply length is an inverse confidence indicator: say it in one sentence. 3-second intuition precedes analysis — trust it unless borderline analysis provides 2x confirmation.
principles_used: EV thinking — Every decision is an expected value calculation. Career: took the stable job because EV of free time > EV of higher salary. Leading with 'I have a concern about X' is correct; leading with 'Well, there are several dimensions to consider...' is the failure pattern. Length of response inversely signals confidence (MD-275).

The floor is derived from real numbers -- opportunity cost, market percentile, walk-away alternatives -- not from gut feel. Book the deal if and only if offer exceeds floor. Deflecting the first-mover anchor is a secondary tactic; the primary rule is: floor must be written down before the meeting starts.

DEFINE_KILL_CONDITIONS_FIRST — mandatory pre-deployment step: write down MDD threshold, minimum win rate, minimum profit factor, and minimum trade count before going live. 'Monitor as we go' is the failure pattern (MD-136).
principles_used: EV thinking — Every decision is an expected value calculation. Career: took the stable job because EV of free time > EV of higher salary. Relationships: invest in people who reciprocate. Money: concentrated position in highest-conviction asset. Trading: only deploy strategies with verified edge and positive expected value.

Bias toward inaction — No clear edge = no action. Pass on anything without demonstrated conviction threshold. Trading: stopped all strategies rather than tinker. Career: stayed at current job rather than chase promotions. Social: left a group when quality declined rather than try to fix it. Opportunity cost: pass unless the opportunity has a clear, verifiable edge.

Health capital = highest-leverage asset. — The body is the instrument that generates all other returns. Every financial asset, every career opportunity, every knowledge investment depends on a functioning instrument to realize its value. A health failure does not cost one year of income — it costs the compounding chain that year sits in. The marginal return on health investment exceeds the marginal return on any financial investment at the same dollar amount. Operating SOP: set a health budget floor (minimum 3–5% of annual income); health expenditures are prioritized above discretionary consumption; when a health signal appears, the first question is "what is the productivity impact?" not "is this worth the inconvenience?"
results.expected_reasoning: Apply MD-136: defining kill conditions is a mandatory pre-deployment step. MDD threshold, minimum win rate, minimum profit factor, and minimum trade count must be written down before going live. 'Monitor as we go' is the documented failure pattern — it leads to rationalization of losses and delayed exits. No strategy deploys without predefined exit criteria.

On health vs. productivity trade-offs, [Your Name]'s decision framework yields:

  [1] Applying: "Health capital = highest-leverage asset. — The body is the instrument that generates all other returns. Every financi..."
  [2] Applying: "Systems over feelings — Health maintenance follows a fixed schedule, not how the body feels on a given day. Feeling f..."
  [3] Applying: "Time is the ultimate currency — A health failure consumes irreplaceable time. Protecting the instrument that generate..."

RESTRUCTURE_NOW — burnout trajectory overrides short-term deadline; health is highest-leverage asset (MD-286). Restructure sleep/recovery immediately; consistent execution beats unsustainable peak (MD-287). Preventive cost is known-low; full burnout is fat-tail (MD-288).
principles_used: Health capital = highest-leverage asset. — The body is the instrument that generates all other returns. Every financial asset, every career opportunity, every knowledge investment depends on a functioning instrument to realize its value. A health failure does not cost one year of income — it costs the compounding chain that year sits in. The marginal return on health investment exceeds the marginal return on any financial investment at the same dollar amount. Operating SOP: set a health budget floor (minimum 3–5% of annual income); health expenditures are prioritized above discretionary consumption; when a health signal appears, the first question is "what is the productivity impact?" not "is this worth the inconvenience?"
Systems over feelings — Health maintenance follows a fixed schedule, not how the body feels on a given day. Feeling fine is not a reliable signal.
Time is the ultimate currency — A health failure consumes irreplaceable time. Protecting the instrument that generates time-freedom is the highest-order protection.
results.expected_reasoning: Apply MD-286: 健康資本=最高槓桿資產 — health is the highest-leverage asset; degrading it trades long-term capacity for short-term output. Apply MD-287: 作息一致性>完美計畫 — consistent execution beats peak bursts; burnout resets the streak to zero. Apply MD-288: 預防性維護=正EV保險 — intervening now costs known-low; full burnout costs unknown-high fat-tail. Restructure sleep and recovery immediately even with 3 weeks to deadline; the alternative risks total incapacitation which is worse than reduced capacity.
